seoTitle: Sunset Strip vs Sunshine Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunset Strip and Sunshine Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunset Strip vs Sunshine Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunset Strip and Sunshine Bay.
heading: Sunset Strip vs Sunshine Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunset Strip, New South Wales and Sunshine Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunset Strip has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Sunshine Bay (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunset Strip or Sunshine Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunset Strip has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Sunshine Bay scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunset Strip scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Sunshine Bay scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunset Strip or Sunshine Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunset Strip has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
